response_excerpt: A low-risk start is to organize your tools and materials before beginning any work. Keep them within easy reach but out of the way to avoid tripping hazards.  Ensure you have good lighting in the area where you're working. Poor visibility can lead to accidents and mistakes.  Before starting any electrical work, always turn off the power at the circuit breaker. This prevents accidental shocks and ensures safety.
